div1.entryFree:
Gripe, vb. 1) to seize, to grasp: Lucr. 319. Lucr. 319 John IV, 2, 190. R2 II, 1, 189. III, 3, 80. H4A V, 1, 57. Oth. III, 3, 421. we live not to be --d by meaner persons, H8 II, 2, 136 (i. e. to join hands with. cf. the subst. in Cymb. I, 6, 106). Absol: many among us can g. as hard as Cassibelan, Cymb. III, 1, 40 (== have as strong hands). Intr.: g. not at earthly joys, Per. I, 1, 49 (== catch at).
2) to pinch, to give pain to: let vultures g. thy guts, Wiv. I, 3, 94 (cf. Gutsgriping). inly sorrow --s his soul, H6C I, 4, 171. Absol.: when --ing grief the heart doth wound, Rom. IV, 5, 128.
